title: Make your changes to the federated database instance storage configuration.

sections.heading: Define a Data Store for a Federated Database Instance
sections.body:
To configure a data store for a federated database instance, you need to
create a storage configuration JSON file that defines your data stores and
maps them to collections you can query. MongoDB provides
federated database instance support for the following data stores:

- [Configure Atlas Data Federation to query data from S3](adf-configuration-file-aws)

- [Azure data stores](adf-configuration-file-azure)

- [Google Cloud Storage Bucket](adf-configuration-file-gcp)

- [Atlas cluster](adf-configuration-file-atlas)

- [HTTP URL](adf-configuration-file-http)

- [Online Archives](adf-configuration-file-oa)

> **Important:** Information in your storage configuration is visible internally at
> MongoDB and stored as operational data to monitor and improve the
> performance of Atlas Data Federation. We recommend that you don't use PII
> (Personally Identifiable Information) in your configurations.

sections.heading: Data Access Types
sections.body:
When you create a federated database instance, you grant Atlas one of the following types of
data access to the AWS (Amazon Web Services) buckets in your AWS (Amazon Web Services) account.

- **read only access:** It cannot make any changes to the data or add new data.

- **read and write access:** It can update existing files, delete files, or
  add new data to the storage.

To access your Atlas clusters, Atlas uses your existing
[Role Based Access Controls](manual/core/authorization). You can view
and edit the acces of your user following the steps in
[Manage Users and Roles on Self-Managed Deployments](manual/tutorial/manage-users-and-roles).

sections.heading: Privilege Actions
sections.body:
Privilege actions are the operations that you can perform on your
federated database instance. You can grant the privilege action through one of the following:

- When you [create or modify](atlas/security-add-mongodb-roles)
  custom roles from the Atlas User Interface

- In the `actions.action` request body parameter when you
  [create](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroupcustomdbrolerole) or
  [update](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupcustomdbrolerole) a custom
  role from the Atlas API (Application Programming Interface)

The following table shows the available federated database instance privilege actions:

| Command                          | Description                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| sqlGetSchemasqlGetSchema         | Retrieve the schema stored for a collection or view, see View Schema Using mongosh for details.                                                                                                         |
| sqlSetSchemasqlSetSchema         | Set or delete the schema for a collection or view, see View Schema Using mongosh for details.                                                                                                           |
| viewAllHistoryviewAllHistory     | Retrieve details about the queries that were run in the past using $queryHistory.                                                                                                                       |
| outToAzureoutToAzure             | Write data from any one of the supported federated database instance stores or multiple supported federated database instance stores to your Azure (Microsoft Azure) Blob Storage container using $out. |
| outToS3outToS3                   | Write data from any one of the supported federated database instance stores or multiple supported federated database instance stores to your S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket using $out.             |
| outToGCPoutToGCP                 | Write data from any one of the supported federated database instance stores or multiple supported federated database instance stores to your Google Cloud Storage bucket using $out.                    |
| storageGetConfigstorageGetConfig | Retrieve your federated database instance storage configuration using the storageGetConfig command.                                                                                                     |
| storageSetConfigstorageSetConfig | Set or update your federated database instance storage configuration using the storageSetConfig command.                                                                                                |

sections.heading: Retrieve Configuration File
sections.body:
To retrieve your Federated Database Instance Configuration File, connect to your instance and use
the following command:

```javascript
use admin
db.runCommand( { "storageGetConfig" : 1 } )
```

The command returns the current federated database instance configuration.

sections.heading: Validate Configuration File
sections.body:
To validate your federated database instance configuration, connect to your instance and use the
following command:

```javascript
use admin
db.runCommand( { "storageValidateConfig" : <config> } )
```

Replace `<config>` with the configuration for the federated database instance.

If the configuration is valid, the command returns the following answer:

```sh
{ "ok" : 1 }
```

If the configuration is invalid, the command returns the list of errors in the
`errs` field:

```sh
{
       "ok" : 1,
       "errs" : [
               "<error>",
               "<error>",
               ...
       ]
}
```

sections.heading: Update your Federated Database Instance Configuration
sections.body:
To update your federated database instance configuration, connect to your instance and use the
following command:

```javascript
use admin
db.runCommand( { "storageSetConfig" : <config> } )
```

Replace `<config>` with the configuration for the federated database instance.

> **Tip:** MongoDB recommends you to [Validate Configuration File](adf-validatestorageconfig) your
> Federated Database Instance configuration before applying any changes.

To update your Federated Database Instance configuration Data stores, data sources, databases,
collections, and views, follow these steps:

- For a visual interface, click Visual.

- For editing directly in the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) file, click JSON Editor.

Visual Editor

sections.heading: Add an Additional Data Source
sections.body:
Corresponds to `stores` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

- Click Add Data Store Sources.

- Select the data source provider you want to configure:
  \- For AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service), continue to step 5 of [Create AWS Data Store From the UI](deploy-s3).
  \- For Google Cloud, continue to step 7 of [Deploy a Federated Database Instance Data Store](deploy-gcp).
  \- For Azure (Microsoft Azure), continue to step 7 of [Create Azure Data Store From the UI](deploy-azure).
  \- For Atlas Cluster, continue to step 7 of [Create Atlas Data Store From the UI](deploy-atlas-datastore).
  \- For HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) data store, continue to step 7 of [Create Atlas Data Store From the UI](deploy-atlas-datastore).
  \- For Atlas Online Archive, continue to step 7 of [Create Online Archive Data Store From the UI](deploy-oa-datastore).

sections.heading: Edit Your Data Source Name
sections.body:
Corresponds to `stores.[n].name` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

- Select your data store source from the list on the left.

- Click the **Edit Store Name**  icon.

- Change the current name in the **Data Store Name** field.

- Click **Done**.

sections.heading: Delete a Data Source
sections.body:
- Select your Data Store source from the list on the left

- Click the **Delete this Store**  icon

- Click the **Delete** button.

**Manage Databases**

sections.heading: Create a New Database
sections.body:
Corresponds to `databases` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

- Click the **Add Database** button on the right-top corner

- Fill in the **Database Name** field

- Fill in the **Collection Name** field

- Click the **Create** button

sections.heading: Edit a Database name
sections.body:
Corresponds to `databases.[n].name` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

- Select your Database from the list.

- Click the **Edit Database Name**  icon.

- Update the **Database Name**.

- Click the **Done** button.

sections.heading: Delete a Database
sections.body:
- Select your Database from the list.

- Click **Delete this Database**  icon.

- Click the **Delete** button.

**Manage Collections**

sections.heading: Create a new Collection in a Database
sections.body:
Corresponds to `databases.[n].collections` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

- Select the Database from where you want to create a Collection

- Click the **Add Collection**  icon

- Fill in the **Collection Name** field

- Click the **Create** button

sections.heading: Edit a Collection name
sections.body:
Corresponds to `databases.[n].collections.[n].name` JSON (Javascript Object Notation)
configuration setting.

- Select your Collection from the list

- Click the **Edit Collection Name**  icon

- Change the current name in the **Collection Name** field for your new name

- Click the **Create** button

sections.heading: Delete a Collection
sections.body:
- Select your Collection from the list

- Click the **Delete this Collection**  icon

- Click the **Delete** button.

**Manage Views**

sections.heading: Create a new View in a Collection
sections.body:
Corresponds to `databases.[n].views` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration setting.

- Select the Collection from where you want to create a View

- Click the **Add View**  icon

- Fill in the **View Name** field

- Fill in the **:manual:\`Pipeline \</core/aggregation-pipeline/>\`** field

- Click the **Create** button

sections.heading: Edit a View name or Pipeline
sections.body:
Corresponds to `databases.[n].views.[n].name` and
`databases.[n].views.[n].pipeline` JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration settings
respectively.

- Select your View from your Collection

- Click the **Edit View Name**  icon

- Change the current name in the **View Name** field for your new name

- Change the current pipeline in the **Pipeline** field for your new pipeline

- Click the **Save** button

sections.heading: Delete a View
sections.body:
- Select your View from the list

- Click the **Delete this View**  icon

- Click the **Delete** button.

JSON Editor

Edit the JSON (Javascript Object Notation) configuration file objects to add, create, or remove
`stores`, `databases`, or `views`

To learn more about the configuration settings of your specific Data Store
provider, see their Configuration File page:

- [Configure Atlas Data Federation to query data from S3](adf-configuration-file-aws)

- [Azure data stores](adf-configuration-file-azure)

- [Google Cloud Storage Bucket](adf-configuration-file-gcp)

- [Atlas cluster](adf-configuration-file-atlas)

- [HTTP URL](adf-configuration-file-http)

- [Online Archives](adf-configuration-file-oa)

mongosh

To learn how to set and manage storage configuration using
[MongoDB Shell (mongosh)](mongodb-shell), see the page
[view](adf-cli).

sections.heading: Generate Wildcard Collections
sections.body:
> **Important:** The Wildcard collections function is available **only** for data sources from
> AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) and Atlas Clusters.

When using Data Sources from [Configure Atlas Data Federation to query data from S3](adf-configuration-file-aws)
or [Atlas cluster](adf-configuration-file-atlas), you can
dynamically set collection names that map to data. To generate a collection
name, specify the wildcard symbol `*` as the value for the collection
name setting in your federated database instance storage configuration.

> **Tip:** You can use the [setStorageConfig](adf-setstorageconfig)
> command to configure the settings for generating wildcard (`*`)
> collections.
